title: HOW TO BEGIN THE DAY? | HG Amitasana Das | GITA LIFE Live
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: Arre Krishna, a warm welcome to all of you for today's Geeta Live session.
The topic for the day is how to start or begin our day. The way we begin our day
there's a saying well begun is half done. So it is very important that we start
our day with early morning victory. We start the day in a right way so that
throughout the day it is a good day.
Good, pleasant and a positive day. A lot depends on how we go about starting our
day. So before we get into the discussion let us all chant few prayers together.
Let's seek the blessings of Srila Prabhupada, Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, Lord
Shri Krishna to bless us so that we can benefit from the insights which they
have so wonderfully shared with us in their writings, in their teachings.
On the basis of which we will be covering today's topic.
So please join me in offering the prayers. You can also fold your hands. You can offer
the prayers along with me.
Nama Om Vishnupadaya, Krishna preshtaya bhutale, Srimate Bhaktivedanta, Svamin itinamine, Namaste
Saraswate Deve.
Gauravani Pracharine, Nirvishesha Shunyavadi, Pasyatya Deshatarine, Jai Shri Krishna Chaitanya,
Prabhu Nityananda, Priy Advaita Gadadhara, Shiva Sadhi Gaurabhakta Vrinda, Namo Mahavadanya,
Krishna Prema Pradayate, Krishna Ya Krishna Chaitanya, Namne Gauratushe Nama, Namo Brahmne Devaya,
Go Bramhne Hitaya Cha, Jagad Hitaaya Krishnaya, Govindaya, Namo Namah.
Hey Krishna, Karuna, Sindhu, Dina Bandhu, Jagatpate, Gopesha, Gopika, Kanta, Radha, Kanta, Namostute, Tapta Kanchan, Gaurangi, Radhe, Vrindavaneshwari
Vrishabhanu Sute Devi, Pranamami Hari Priye
Vanchya Kalpat Rubhyascha, Kripa Sindhu Bhya Evacha
Patitanam Pavnebhyo, Vaishnavebhyo, Namo Namaha
Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare
Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare
So there are many ways, there are many things which we can do to start the day well.
But we will try to cover three of those many important ways.
So in other words, there are many other things which can be done.
Which can be incorporated in our morning schedule.
But we will try to cover three of them today, need not be the most important.
The first aspect which is very very important for us to keep in mind, to start the day on
a good note.
Many of us are very conscious of Shtubha.
Shtubha is a very important thing.
Shtubha and Ashubha, auspiciousness and inauspiciousness.
When people go for a walk, suddenly they find the cat passing the way, they stop.
It is considered as inauspicious.
Recently there was Dhanteras and I was told that in Jaipur there is one temple where lines
and lies of people lined up with new cars.
Wanting to get the prayers done so that they can have auspicious beginning with the new
car.
So everyone is concerned about Shubha and Ashubha.
Our scriptures give us a very nice perspective of what is auspicious and what is inauspicious.
In one sense, the so-called inauspiciousness.
Inauspiciousness.
Inauspiciousness.
Inauspiciousness.
There is a lot of auspiciousness in this world and so-called inauspiciousness.
Shtubha and Ashubha, our scriptures say, it is in one sense Manodharma.
It is a mental concoction.
We brandish something as Shtubha and we label something as Ashubha.
But according to scriptures Shtubha, the most auspicious is that activity in the form of
inauspiciousness.
Inauspiciousness.
That activity which brings us closer to Krishna or to God.
That activity which makes us remember God.
And what is Ashubha?
What is inauspiciousness?
That activity which takes us away from God.
Which makes us forget God.
Even the so-called pious activity, if it makes us forget God or takes us away from God, it's
inauspiciousness.
Inauspiciousness.
But it is Ashubha.
So with this definition in mind, with this understanding in mind, with this perspective,
the best way to start our day is engaging in spiritual activities, which takes us closer
to Krishna.
So one very, very important thing.
In fact.
The first thing which we should do, no doubt when we get up, we can always, morning ablutions,
washing our face, brushing our teeth, immediately after that, we should offer prayers.
In the morning, after taking bath, in all ISKCON temples, we have something called as
the morning prayers, Mangal Aarti.
Generally it happens at 4.15 am.
In the description of this video today, I have shared with you a link where you can
download three versions of this Mangal Aarti prayers.
Each one of you can also do this Mangal Aarti prayers at your place.
Just play the audio.
It is very nicely done.
We have the intention of keeping this purpose in mind.
We have many folk hostels all over the city, in different cities in India and even outside
where young boys stay and we use this audio.
The audio is played.
It is a lead and follow method.
It also has devotees who are playing Mridanga.
Kartal.
So.
the Mangala Aarti. These prayers are glorifying the spiritual master Srila Prabhupada. That's
the best way to start the day where we offer our prayers to the spiritual master. If possible
you can also do a small Aarti. It could be as simple as offering some incense or offering
a small lamp or offering some flowers. So there are three versions. One version is a
full version which is for about 37 minutes depending on how much time you have in the
morning because everyone is differently placed with different schedules, with different commitments.
So we have a full version which is 37 minutes which has the Mangala Aarti, which has Guru
Puja, which has Tulsi Aarti, which has Narasimha Puja and ends with Kirtan. There is a shorter
version which has a full version which is 37 minutes which has the Mangala Aarti, which
is for 19 minutes in which the Guru Puja is not there, the Tulsi Puja is there, Narasimha
Aarti is there and the Mangala Aarti is there. And there is another version which is the
Guru Puja and the Kirtan which is for 12 minutes. Guru Puja is offering prayers to the lotus
feet of the spiritual master. So you can download these prayers, the audios, it's in mp3 format
and you can use them.
To start the day by offering the Mangala Aarti. I have also shared with you in the description
below a link of the prayers which are there in the Bhagavatam of exalted devotees like
Prahlad Maharaj, like Dhruva Maharaj, like Kunti Maharani. They are being nicely rendered
and chanted by the devotees with some nice melodious background music, very soothing.
You can also play these audios when you get up in the morning while you are getting ready
for the work. You can play these audios, Purush Sukta prayers. You can have these prayers.
It will create all auspiciousness in the home. It will create a very nice spiritual environment.
And for sure, yes, after we do these prayers, we can do the most important prayer which
is chanting the holy names of the lord. Chanting is also a prayer and the prayer is, O Lord
Shri Krishna, O energy of Krishna, please engage me in your service. Right now, we are
engaged in service of Maya, service of our own mind, service of our own senses. We engage
in the service of false masters.

We cannot avoid service. And this Hare Krishna Maha Mantra is prayer to Lord Sri Krishna and His energy to engage us in His Seva, in His service. So we chant the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra, we offer these prayers and to begin the day offering prayers, chanting the holy names of the Lord is the best way to start the day.
In fact the first thing we should try to do before we get caught up with our other duties, other responsibilities, we should do this most important duty. They are saying the most important thing
is to offer prayers. Apologies for the technical hitch. So as I mentioned, the best way to start the day is to offer these prayers.
And these prayers I have shared the link with you, all of you can download and benefit from these prayers. I also mentioned that many times we offer prayers which is
more of asking favours from the Lord. We all have some challenges which we are encountering
in life, some difficulties, some rough phase and the prayer is all about seeking the blessings
of the Lord so that we can tide through those difficult times. The best is that we simply
offer prayers glorifying the Lord, following the footsteps of the prayers which are offered
by great devotees of the Lord. That's why it is best that we can take example, we can
learn from the prayers which are mentioned in the Srimad Bhagavatam. Prahlad Maharaj
is offering prayers, Bhishma is offering prayers, Dhruva Maharaj is offering prayers,
Ambarish Maharaj is offering prayers, Gajendra is offering prayers, Kunti Maharani is offering
prayers and we can learn from such prayers as to what are the prayers which we should
offer.
What are the prayers which will please the Lord? What are the prayers which will bring
us closer to the Lord? And when the Lord is pleased, all our material needs also are automatically
taken care of. Lord is all-knowing, He is there in our heart. He is a well-wisher of
the devotees, in fact for everyone. Once there was a
king who was going around in his kingdom in a procession. At times he would come down
from the chariot and would walk for a while. And that particular day it was very hot and
he had very special shoes, embroidered shoes and while he was walking, meeting people, he suddenly

they thought that it is best if you could somehow do some repairs

We seek out of ignorance because we don't know what is our real self-interest, what is our real good and at times out of ignorance we ask for some petty things.
So best is when we offer prayers, we keep it general. It should be more of glorifying the Lord, thanking the Lord, expressing our gratitude and not be very specific.
In fact, I have shared this in one of our earlier sessions. In case we get tempted to ask specific prayers, let's say throughout the year you have been preparing for an exam,
a critical, important exam.
And today is the day, the most important day where it's a make or break day.
If you clear the exam, it takes you to a next level and it's very important for you to clear the exam and you approach the Lord and pray to the Lord.
If you are specific in offering prayers, a saving grace is we can ask the Lord after we have asked specific benediction.
If you so desire.
O Lord, if you so desire, let me pass this exam.
Because the Lord is our well-wisher.
Many times what we think is the best for us may not be the best for us.
Maybe the Lord has higher plans, better plans, something better in store for us.
So we don't be specific in our prayers.
We follow in the footsteps of great devotees.
We take, we learn from the prayers what they have offered.
I have also shared with you all a link in the description of this video.
Some authorized prayers. In fact, there are three links.
Some authorized prayers. One link, one PDF is a compilation of 21 authorized prayers.
From different authorized scriptures like Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavatam, prayers to spiritual master, prayers to the Lord.
So there are 21 prayers.
So you can keep a printout with you and you can recite those prayers looking at the printout.
And in due course of time, few days, you will be able to memorize those prayers.
And then you don't have to have a printout.
You can fold your hands and you can offer the prayers.
You can offer the Sanskrit part of it, the shlokas.
Or you can even offer the translation, the meaning of that prayers.
Best is if you can do both the translation and the original prayer, the verse.
And if you find it difficult to pronounce the Sanskrit shlokas, you can just offer the translation part of it.
There is a shorter version also, another PDF which has about 8 to 10 prayers.
Simple prayers, two line, three line prayers.
You can also take a printout and use those as prayers which we can offer to the Lord.
To sum up, the first point, the first aspect, start the day with prayer.
We can, while we are getting up, while we are getting ready, we can always put the prayers which are
playing as like Dhruva Maharaj prayers, Kunti Maharaj.
I have shared with you the audio links.
And once we are ready, we can fold our hands, go in front of the Lord and do a small aarti.
We can have the Mangal Aarti audio track.
We can offer that aarti to the Lord.
We can offer some prayers.
I have shared authorized prayers, 21 prayers or the shorter version of that.
And when we offer these prayers,
we will get an idea of what kind of prayers we should offer.
And after offering these prayers, the Mangal Aarti,
we can always offer the most important prayer,
which is to chant the holy names of the Lord, to chant the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra.
So if we start the day with these prayers, remembering the Lord, thanking the Lord,
expressing our gratitude to the Lord, glorifying the Lord,
the Lord will be with us throughout the day,
helping us, guiding us, taking charge of us.
So that is first thing which I would like all of you to consider
and keep in mind when you plan the schedule as to how to start the day.
The second aspect which I wanted to cover, which is again very very important,
is that in life, we will have good times, we will have bad times.
We will come across good people, we will come across bad people,
cruel people.
Prabhupada gives an example, if you walk in a garden,
typically you will find two kinds of plies,
or insects.
One is the honey bee.
It moves around in the garden, hovers around the flowers,
and collects the nectar from different flowers, goes flower to flower and connects the nectar.
So that's the honey bee.
In the same garden, there is another kind of fly, the house fly,
which looks for filth and garbage in the garden and goes and hovers around it.
Where is stool, where is filth, where is garbage, and you will find that house fly hovering around it.
Now, in every garden, even if it is well maintained, there is some crap, there is some garbage,
maybe in one corner, piled together, there is also some nice, beautiful, aromatic flowers blossoming.
And there are two kinds of flies.
The honey bee, which focuses on the aroma, the flowers which are blossoming,
and there is another kind of fly which is this.
So, while we are becoming devotees, it is very important that we have the right attitude.
Our behaviour is exemplary.
Prabhupada said, people will judge me, people will judge your spiritual master, seeing your behaviour.
So, many times, by default, because of our upbringing or because of conditioning,
we can have a very negative outlook to life.
We can be overly focused on what is not okay.
We can be overly focused on the problems and challenges which I have to encounter in my spiritual life.
And as a result, we can be always complaining, blaming, playing the victim card.
So, in the morning, I had also shared in the previous session that
on an average day, an average person thinks 60,000 thoughts.
And most of the thoughts are random and unnecessary.
And they have also figured out that around 77%, on an average,
77% of the thoughts are negative and counterproductive.
So, in other words, we invariably, unless we proactively work on ourselves,
we invariably have a tendency to get focused on what is not okay,
what is, some things which are negative, some things, the challenges which we are going through.
We get focused on that.
And all the blessings, all the good things in life, we take it for granted.
So, the second principle which is very, very important,
which we should definitely try to cultivate,
and the principle is attitude of gratitude.
I repeat.
Attitude of gratitude.
I'll tell you about my own life in the morning, my own schedule.
In the morning when I get up, even before taking bath,
I sit near the window, fresh air,
and I have made list of wonderful things which Lord has blessed me in my life.
And that list keeps expanding.
For example, in my life, I am blessed to have Shila Prabhupada as my spiritual master.
I am blessed to have opportunity to chant the holy names of Krishna daily.
I am blessed to have the knowledge of Bhagavad Gita and Srimad Bhagavatam.
I am blessed to have nice prasadam to eat.
There are lot of people dying of starvation.
They may have food to eat, but here I have opportunity to honour daily sanctified prasadam,
which is offered to the Lord, the remnants of that food,
which is spiritually uplifting.
I am blessed to have that prasadam.
I am blessed to have nice parents.
I am blessed to live in association of devotees.
I am blessed to have nice opportunity to grow in spiritual life.

because suddenly if you start thinking,
you know, you may forget something.
So this list keeps expanding.
If you develop an attitude of gratitude,
you will always find there is so much more to be grateful for.
The reverse is also true.
If we get into a negative cycle,
if we start complaining, blaming, playing the victim game,
then we will have more opportunities to play that game.
